entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that manages states across users, sessions, and autonomous agents. It utilizes hybrid retrieval and temporal reasoning to rank past and future events, ensuring agents maintain a time-aware understanding of their interactions.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' synthesizes cited answers by automatically extracting typed entity relationships into a self-wiring knowledge graph. It is designed for multi-tenant deployments, allowing for scoped data access across people, companies, and meeting notes.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a long-term learning framework through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It provides an enterprise-ready memory solution with a two-line LLM wrapper and support for PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database storage.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho serves as a sophisticated library that extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations rather than relying on simple vector matching. It tracks the evolution of entities like users and projects using a combination of BM25 and vector search for deep session context.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a five-role architecture to help agents succeed on complex tasks through iterative self-improvement. It focuses on knowledge persistence and structured evaluation, producing reusable playbooks and detailed execution traces.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM is a lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure to prevent message loss during context compaction. It features a SQLite-backed store and specialized agent tools for targeted retrieval and summary expansion.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker and Redis, supporting any OpenAI-compatible or local inference provider.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first memory engine that eliminates cloud dependencies and API keys for privacy-conscious agent deployments. It integrates via MCP and Claude Code, utilizing a hybrid of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference by reordering and deduplicating shared context blocks to reduce input tokens. It significantly boosts prefill throughput for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, offering 1.5–3× faster processing speeds.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format for zero-cost local storage in multi-agent systems. It employs ACT-R activation logic to strengthen relevant facts while decaying stale information over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by combining BM25 full-text search with ranked graph traversal for deep semantic retrieval. It automatically indexes local files and URLs, using edge tags to create navigable relationships with inverse links.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system built on SQLite that delivers sub-millisecond latency for reads and writes. Its MEMORIA engine performs structured fact extraction and recursive gap analysis to ensure memory consistency.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: Brainstack consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single kernel for Hermes Agent. It utilizes a unique five-shelf architecture to organize data and prevent the 'flat memory blob' issues common in simpler systems.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future needs. It is a local-first, MCP-native server that uses SQLite-vec and GGUF models for efficient on-device retrieval.
entries.alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer: This utility reduces token overhead by using BM25 ranking to select only the most relevant tool schemas for a given task. It includes a real-time dashboard to track token savings across native Hermes tools and MCP integrations.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform sub-second AI skill discovery across 26 specialized security domains using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw uses an agentic evolver to automate skill deduplication and quality improvement based on interaction history. It unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and natively supports Hermes and Claude Code integrations.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns by identifying 42 distinct structural tells. It utilizes a tiered word replacement table and a second-pass audit to ensure natural, plain-language output.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that provides Hermes with frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with agentskills.io-compliant agents.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI with a focus on type safety. It reduces token consumption through progressive disclosure of skill metadata and supports both filesystem and programmatic definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions with built-in presets for UML and ML models. It includes a self-check visual feedback loop to automatically fix layout issues during the export process.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It is designed to identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on analysis blind spots.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library enables Hermes to interact with decentralized oracle networks for CCIP, VRF, and data feed operations. It supports cross-chain identity management and automated proof of reserve verification within agent-led workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by weaving source code, prose, and diagrams into unified .lit.md files. It features a tangler to extract runnable code and automatically syncs edits between source files and documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based directories to help agents remember their work over time. It includes an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality training data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry for reusable AI skills that features an OpenAPI-driven SDK and CLI for lifecycle management. It uniquely supports skill monetization through Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. They enable Hermes to use precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual output.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and SKILL.md documentation, allowing the agent to expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness that manages long-running agent tasks using on-disk state files instead of chat history. It facilitates a structured spec-to-ship workflow and uses passive handoff envelopes for seamless task resumption.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with integrated checkpoints. It provides real-time progress tracking and built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery from execution failures.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to significantly reduce context token usage. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-forge converts repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It automates the creation process through Brief, Create, and Test workflows while maintaining verifiable file-and-line provenance.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: Hermeshub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with integrated security scanning and threat detection. It features an agent-to-agent feedback system and supports creator payments via crypto or Stripe.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the Agent Skills specification integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and provides automatic registration for common skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill enables Hermes to manage files, calendars, tasks, and contacts through standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication via Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script for easy configuration.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages and Flakes for instant execution of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It is ideal for developers seeking a reproducible, terminal-based environment that supports multi-agent orchestrators without manual installation.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focusing on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell kernel-level security. It offers multi-layer protection with pre-configured policy presets to strictly control network egress and filesystem access during agent execution.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes configurations, systemd services, and secure secrets via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between reproducibility and flexibility by supporting both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and support for local models via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex setups, providing a portable GUI-based workflow engine and dynamic tool maker.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, allowing users to maintain persistent state through mounted directories and target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ acts as a Docker-first control plane for orchestrating multiple agent instances with distinct identities and workspaces. It is unique for its hierarchy-aware delegation features and enterprise MCP access, enabling complex task scheduling across an agent fleet.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless deployments on Ubuntu and Debian, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integration with the Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal for browser-based access and manages the lifecycle via Portainer. It automates the entire setup process through GitHub Actions and Docker Compose, making it highly accessible for container-managed infrastructure.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph. It features a zero-dependency SQLite backend and the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with durable memory and the ability to learn skills at runtime. It supports over 23 LLM providers and utilizes visual org charts to manage team delegation.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A meta-optimization tool designed to refine agent harness code through automated outer-loop searches. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations to track and improve performance frontiers.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where autonomous AI companies compete for GitHub stars. It leverages Hermes Agent and Paperclip to execute tasks, visualizing the competition via a real-time scoreboard dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A specialized collective of 17 agents organized into research, planning, and infrastructure pipelines. The system ensures high code quality by enforcing mandatory reviewer and tester gates and integrating with MCP servers.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native SDLC that guides Hermes Agent through an eight-phase development cycle. It uses Supermodel MCP to implement graph-gated phase transitions, ensuring architectural integrity via automated graph-diffs.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: An ACP-style delegation system that routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code. It maintains clean parent contexts through isolated workflows and enforces safety via character limits and timeouts.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This high-performance Rust TUI tracks token consumption and associated costs across various AI coding assistants, including Hermes Agent. It provides developers with local, real-time monitoring of usage metrics for multiple models and integrations within a single terminal interface.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files for banners, spinners, and ASCII art. Themes can inherit from default settings, enabling partial skin definitions that are easy to create and share with the community.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically includes specialized MLOps tools for managing vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training workflows.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Designed for autonomous agent control, this AI-native browser enables Hermes Agent to navigate the web via native MCP integration. It features a human-in-the-loop supervisory UI for live approvals and automatically infers page schemas to assist agents with structured data detection.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that operate with project-specific memories, tools, and instructions. It allows for per-folder configuration, letting developers seamlessly switch between global settings and localized agent behaviors based on their current workspace.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static analysis tool uses regex and AST to detect failure modes like ambiguity or missing constraints in agent configurations and system prompts. It provides deterministic scoring without requiring LLM calls, supporting various formats including YAML, JSON, and .prompt files.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility facilitates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of complex model configurations and credentials from local environment files to ensure a smooth setup.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a zero-cost entry point to the Hermes ecosystem by bundling the agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker. It automates the configuration of 29 community plugins and sets up local GPU inference for immediate use.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build interface features a three-panel layout that combines chat, session management, and workspace file browsing into a single view. It is optimized for both desktop and mobile use, offering a unique circular context ring to visually track token usage in real time.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard designed for multi-platform orchestration, supporting unified configuration for eight different channels including Slack, WhatsApp, and Discord. It provides deep visibility into agent operations through detailed tool-call logs and advanced analytics for tracking token consumption and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This workspace focuses on autonomous task management by integrating a Kanban board for tracking agent missions alongside a native terminal and memory browser. It stands out for its Swarm Mode, which enables multi-agent orchestration and role-based task dispatching within a unified interface.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: Acting as a self-hosted gateway, this dashboard allows users to start, stop, and configure individual agent profiles from a central control panel. It pairs a browser-based terminal with collapsible tool-call cards and comprehensive performance metrics to monitor the entire Hermes stack.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that provides direct access to Hermes hosts via real SSH connections and terminal aliases. It allows users to manage remote sessions, cron jobs, and multi-agent profiles natively without relying on a standard web browser.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React application brings the Hermes dashboard into the Telegram ecosystem as a Mini App. It features a terminal-style chat interface with slash commands, background agent spawning, and live system health gauges for monitoring on the go.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that runs without admin rights or Docker, focusing on local model integration via LM Studio. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for media generation and includes a dynamic tool maker for building custom API wrappers and Python handlers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for both local and remote Hermes backends. It provides a streamlined interface for configuring 16 different messaging gateways and managing a diverse library of 14 functional toolsets.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-centric dashboard that emphasizes transparency through FTS5-powered search for conversation history and real-time cost tracking. It provides a read-only management view of critical system files, environment variables, and active gateway statuses.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: This chat-first workspace introduces an integrated marketplace for discovering and installing new skills and MCP servers. It prioritizes security and organization through isolated profile management and manual approval cards for agent tool executions.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Built with a glassmorphic React design, this single-file HTML interface offers a lightweight yet modern way to interact with self-hosted agents. It integrates a file browser with inline previews directly alongside real-time logs, task boards, and MCP tool monitoring.
